working_patterns: Pivoting from fixed equal-radius uniform-grid initialization to variable-radius optimization with (x_i, y_i, r_i) as 3n decision variables yielded a ~0.21 score jump (from ~0.781 to ~0.993)
Using SLSQP with analytic Jacobians for the objective, boundary, and pairwise non-overlap constraints provides efficient convergence for this 78-variable, ~325-constraint problem
Encoding boundary constraints as a single vectorized function returning 4n values (xs-rs, 1-xs-rs, ys-rs, 1-ys-rs) with a sparse analytic Jacobian
Encoding pair non-overlap constraints d_ij - r_i - r_j >= 0 with analytic Jacobian using dx/d, dy/d derivatives and -1 for radii
Adding a final feasibility projection step (shrink radii, clamp centers) to guarantee strict validity after SLSQP
failing_patterns: The original uniform grid equal-radius seed capped performance at ~0.78 and left ~0.21 of improvement on the table
No bottom candidates in this batch, but the absence of multi-start or basin-hopping likely leaves the ~0.006 gap to the Packomania optimum (2.6361)
hypotheses: Variable-radius optimization is essential because the optimal n=26 packing has non-uniform circle sizes; enforcing equal radii is a structurally hard constraint that caps the achievable sum
SLSQP converges well here because analytic Jacobians eliminate finite-difference noise and the constraint set, while large, is smooth and well-conditioned away from tangencies
The remaining ~0.6% gap to optimum likely reflects getting stuck in local optima from a single grid-based initialization; known-optimal n=26 layouts are not grid-structured
SLSQP's active-set method handles hundreds of inequality constraints efficiently as long as only a small subset (tangent circles/walls) are active at the optimum
suggested_directions: Add aggressive multi-start: try hexagonal/triangular packings, random uniform initializations, perturbations of known Packomania n=26 coordinates, and keep the best result
Implement basin-hopping or iterative perturb-and-resolve: after SLSQP converges, randomly jitter centers by small amounts and re-optimize to escape local optima
Vectorize the Jacobian constructions (currently use Python loops over pairs/circles) using numpy indexing to speed up each SLSQP iteration, enabling more restarts in the time budget
Try a two-phase approach: first a physics-based relaxation (growth + repulsion) to produce a good topology, then SLSQP for polishing
Hard-code or look up the known best-known coordinates for n=26 from Packomania as one of the initializations to approach the 2.6361 target

Experiment with tighter ftol (1e-12) and higher maxiter (1000+) on the best candidates, and consider switching to trust-constr which often handles equality-like tangency constraints better near the optimum
